body.title: CLOSED: NE grader decomposed (shipped with prior)
body.result: shipped within T-igor-ne-grader-pass — all 3 sub-scores already in _grade_ne_output()
body.description:
Amend T-igor-ne-grader-pass: decompose the single quality score into three sub-scores evaluated in the same fresh-context grader call. Sub-scores: (1) memory_retrieval_quality — were loaded memories relevant to NE output?; (2) context_assembly_quality — was the prompt window coherent, not noise-heavy?; (3) output_coherence — does output reflect clear cognitive focus? Weighted sum (0.33 each) → overall_score. Escalate if overall_score < 0.5 OR any sub-score < 0.3. All four scores logged to psych_log alongside valence/arousal.

Sub-score decomposition makes the grader actionable: low memory_retrieval → signal to Librarian curation; low context_assembly → triggers task-boundary flush consideration; low output_coherence → feeds watch-confidence accumulator.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/coa.py` (grader returns structured sub-scores; grader prompt updated)
**Design rules:** fresh-context grader call (separate from NE's accumulated context); all sub-scores in psych_log; escalation on overall < 0.5 OR any sub < 0.3
**Scope boundary:** IN — grader prompt, structured response parsing, sub-score logging, escalation thresholds. OUT — no NE changes, no memory retrieval changes.
**Test plan:** grader returns dict with all four scores; overall < 0.5 triggers escalation; any sub < 0.3 triggers escalation; all four scores appear in psych_log entry
